Assignments.ParameterValue: Thanks everyone. This session is specifically about locking the Daimaru rescue plan before Monday's sync. We already know the three objections; now we need a response strong enough that Daisuke can carry it into the procurement meeting next week.
Daimaru Demo Flow - SAP Connector, Store Ops, and 45-Day Pilot

The demo story should start with what Daimaru cares about: stock visibility across flagship floors and the speed of exception handling for store managers. I can show that with our prebuilt SAP connector and a Japanese-language operational dashboard, which lets us position the initial implementation as a 45-day pilot instead of a full transformation.

That shorter pilot story also helps because I can point to what we are doing in Mumbai. Two of my SMB customers accepted a phased deployment this week once we stopped selling the entire roadmap up front. The same logic should make Daimaru's procurement team less nervous about the services line.

Commercially, I recommend a 7 percent year-one concession paired with a flat year-two renewal and a 40-thousand services credit only if they sign by March 20. That keeps us inside the margin guardrail from Wednesday and gives Hiroshi something concrete to trade for urgency.

I agree, and I want the executive-sponsor section to sound outcome-based, not apologetic. Hiroshi, I can offer a direct call with the Daimaru CIO and commit to a monthly steering review for the first quarter after signature. That signals commitment without promising custom product work.

That is exactly what they need. Keisuke Mori told me yesterday that the customer's internal debate is no longer whether they like us; it is whether we look decisive enough to trust during rollout. A 45-day pilot, a 7 percent year-one concession, and a sponsor cadence gives them a coherent reason to stay with us.

I will also add a runbook slide that shows how we cap implementation work at 95 thousand unless Daimaru asks for non-standard store integrations. That protects us from scope creep and makes the lower number credible.
Final concession recommendation for Monday: 7% year-one, flat year-two, 40K services credit if signed by Mar 20.
Drafting the executive sponsor paragraph now: monthly steering review, CIO call next week, and sponsor visibility through pilot.

Great. Final recap: Wei owns the demo and runbook, Aisha owns the pricing language, Mei Lin owns sponsor framing, and I will package all three into the customer memo tonight. By Monday's week-two sync we should be ready to ask the Facilitator to turn this into a formal save proposal.
Summarize the Daimaru save-plan workshop into three workstreams: demo, pricing, and executive-sponsor commitment.
